evals.prompt: Write a PRD for adding multi-language support (i18n) to our React web app. We currently only support English. Our analytics show 30% of users have non-English browser locales. We want to support Spanish, French, and Portuguese initially.
evals.expected_output: Should produce a lightweight agile PRD with clear problem statement backed by data, user stories prioritized by MoSCoW, non-goals (e.g., not RTL languages yet), and success metrics.
assertions: Problem statement includes the 30% data point as justification
Goals are measurable (e.g., support 3 languages, reduce bounce rate for non-English users)
Non-goals explicitly state what's deferred (RTL, CJK, machine translation)
User stories cover content translation, language switching, locale detection
MoSCoW prioritization distinguishes must-have from nice-to-have
Constraints mention things like URL strategy, SEO, content workflow
Keeps the PRD concise (not a 10-page waterfall spec)

evals.prompt: Write a technical RFC for migrating our user authentication from session-based to JWT tokens. We have 200K active users and can't afford downtime during migration.
evals.expected_output: Should produce an RFC with the zero-downtime constraint front and center, propose a gradual migration strategy, present alternatives, and identify specific risks.
assertions: Zero-downtime requirement shapes the entire proposal (not just a footnote)
Proposes a gradual migration (dual-auth period, not big bang)
Presents alternatives (session-based improvements, OAuth provider, etc.)
Addresses security implications of JWT (token revocation, expiry strategy)
Includes data migration plan for existing sessions
Has open questions that invite reviewer input
Links to feature-planning for implementation task breakdown
